summary.intent: Fix review comments on PR #484 in the moltis repo, specifically addressing test safety issues with /tmp paths, and push the changes.
summary.outcome: Fixed tests in parse.rs that used /tmp as skill_dir (which could cause false positives if /tmp/_meta.json exists), committed and pushed the changes.

repo: Skills parsing in crates/skills/src/parse.rs has a slug resolution fallback that reads _meta.json from skill_dir — tests must use a controlled temp dir, not /tmp, to avoid environment-dependent behavior.
Frontmatter slug is tried before _meta.json fallback; if frontmatter slug is present and invalid, _meta.json is not consulted.
PR review is done via GitHub on moltis-org/moltis, PR #484.

code.finding: test_invalid_name_with_invalid_slug_rejected uses /tmp as skill_dir but is safe because frontmatter slug is tried first — however it was fixed for consistency.
test_missing_frontmatter and test_missing_closing_delimiter also reference /tmp but fail before reaching resolve_name_or_slug, so they are safe from the _meta.json pollution risk.

workflow: Unresolved PR review threads can be fetched and resolved programmatically via GitHub API during the fix workflow.
After fixing tests, the pattern is: run affected tests, then commit and push in one step.

friction: A previous push was already complete when the user asked to 'commit and push', requiring a status check to confirm nothing was pending — minor communication overhead.
Multiple tests used /tmp as skill_dir, requiring a grep pass to find all instances rather than fixing just the one flagged in review.
